voiceover: Mascot's out front waving at traffic. Inside, the chat widget blinks — but nobody's home. An accountant comes back from vacation. Analytics say 40 visitors. Inbox says zero leads. Every single one had a question. Not one got an answer. Your website isn't a gap when you're offline — it's a permanent, invisible leak. Every night. Every weekend. Every vacation. Fix it before you check your stats again.
title: The Week Your Website Talked to No One
concept: Open on a sweating mascot costume waving at passing cars in a sun-baked parking lot — absurd, committed, and completely ignored. Cut inside: a chat widget blinks with a typing indicator, but no message ever sends. Nobody's there. Then: an accountant walks back into her office, drops her suitcase, opens her analytics dashboard — dozens of visitors. Opens her inbox — silence. The gut-punch lands. The voiceover reframes the moment: this isn't a vacation problem, it's a permanent structural leak. Close on a calm, answered chat — the AI assistant that never left.
script: BEAT 1 — HOOK (0–3s): Wide shot of mascot costume in blazing parking lot heat, waving frantically at indifferent traffic. Inside the office window behind them, a chat widget blinks with a fake typing indicator — no message ever arrives. VO: 'Mascot suffers, fake typing online.' BEAT 2 — IMPLICATION (3–10s): Cut to an accountant dropping her luggage at her desk. She opens her laptop. Analytics: 40 visitors this week. She opens her inbox: zero messages. Her face says everything. VO: 'An accountant comes back from vacation. 40 visitors. Zero leads. Every single one had a question. Not one got an answer.' BEAT 3 — TWIST / ESCALATION (10–18s): Slow zoom on the empty inbox. VO: 'Your website isn't a gap when you're offline — it's a permanent, invisible leak. Every night. Every weekend. Every vacation.' BEAT 4 — PAYOFF / CTA (18–23s): Cut to the same website — chat widget now active, a visitor question answered instantly, no human required. VO: 'Fix it before you check your stats again.' On-screen text: 'Create your AI assistant — fenrik.chat'
image_prompts: Photorealistic vertical 9:16 portrait. Exterior of a small business office on a bright, sun-drenched day. A person wearing a full foam mascot costume — oversized cartoon head, colorful suit — stands in the parking lot waving enthusiastically at passing cars that ignore them completely. Sweat visibly soaking through the costume fabric at the neck. Through the large glass window behind the mascot, a desktop monitor is faintly visible with a chat widget interface showing a blinking ellipsis typing indicator — but no message appears. The contrast between the absurd outdoor effort and the silent, unanswered digital interior is the core of the image. Natural daylight, candid composition, warm colors.

Photorealistic vertical 9:16 portrait. A woman in her early 40s — professional, slightly tired from travel — stands at her desk in a small tidy office. A rolling suitcase sits beside her chair, still packed. She leans over an open laptop, her eyes scanning the screen with a look of quiet dread. The laptop screen shows a website analytics dashboard with a visible spike in visitor numbers — the screen is readable in structure but no specific text is legible. She simultaneously glances at an open email client tab showing an empty inbox. Body language: the slow realization of something missed. Warm natural window light, candid and believable.

Photorealistic vertical 9:16 portrait. Extreme close-up on the accountant's laptop screen from over her shoulder. The analytics graph shows a clear spike in site visits over the past week. Next to it, her email inbox panel is completely empty — no unread messages, no contact form submissions, nothing. Her hand rests still on the trackpad, not clicking. The contrast between the visitor spike and the empty inbox is the visual story. Soft natural office light, shallow depth of field, her blurred profile visible at the edge of frame.

Photorealistic vertical 9:16 portrait. The same small business website shown on a desktop monitor — but now the chat widget in the corner is active and glowing. A visitor question appears in the chat bubble. An instant AI response populates below it, clean and helpful. No human is seated at the desk — the chair is empty, the office is quiet, lights are low suggesting after hours. The chat continues without anyone present. The mood shifts from dread to quiet relief. Natural ambient light from the monitor screen, calm and grounded composition.

Not one contact form submission. Every one of those visitors had a real question — about tax deadlines, service packages, whether she was taking new clients. Her website had static text and a form nobody filled out. This is not a vacation problem. It is a structural one. Leads do not wait for business hours. Most small businesses have no way to answer when they are not there — and they never see the visitors they lose.

Not one contact form submission. Every one of those visitors had a real question — about tax deadlines, service packages, whether she was taking new clients. Her website had static text and a form nobody filled out. This is not a vacation problem. It is a structural one. Leads do not wait for business hours. Most small businesses have no way to answer when they are not there — and they never see the visitors they lose.

Most small business websites have the same silent flaw: they attract visitors, then offer them nothing. No answers. No interaction. Just a contact form that sits empty. The visitors leave. The business owner never knows. This is not a gap that only happens during vacations or weekends. It happens every night, every public holiday, every time the team is on a call or with a client. The leads do not disappear loudly. They just quietly move on to whoever answered first.
